title: Program Creation: Compilation, Linking and Relocation
summary: Covers the stages of program creation (compilation, linking), object file structure, the relocation and external-reference problems, and four solutions to the load-time relocation problem.
content_points: Compilation: source → object file (each module compiled independently, starting at address 0).
Object file: header + entry point table + external reference table + code + relocation dictionary.
Linking: assign start addresses (cumulative sizes); add relocation constants; resolve external references.
Load-time relocation solutions: (1) relocation info in executable; (2) relocation register; (3) position-independent code; (4) pagination (program believes it starts at 0).
Position-independent code: uses only relative addresses; portable but hard to write.
learning_objectives: Describe the compilation and linking stages
Explain the contents of an object file
Compare four solutions to the load-time relocation problem
